popularStory.headline: John Lennard, Biography
popularStory.description: John Lennard, as the eldest son, inherited his father's house and lands. He became a prosperous lawyer at Lincoln's Inn. Under Henry VIII he became a justice of the peace, prothonotary (clerk of court …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir John Page Leonard, III, High Barrister at Lincoln Inn, Sheriff of Kent於1508年出生在英國英格蘭肯特設維寧時，他的父親Sir John Lennard, II, Sheriff of Kent II29歲，母親Lady Katherine Elizabeth Weston27歲。他於1526年與Elizabeth Harmon在英國肯特設維寧聖 波托爾福也斯 邱奇結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和7個女兒的父母。他於1570年住在英國英格蘭肯特。他在英國英格蘭倫敦被列為lawyer, barrister at the treasury’s office,  lincoln’s inn - exclusive society of lawyers。他去世於1590年3月12日在英國肯特設維寧享年82歲，埋葬在英國肯特設維寧聖 波托爾福也斯 邱奇。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir John Page Leonard, III, High Barrister at Lincoln Inn, Sheriff of Kent于1508年出生在英国英格兰肯特設維寧时，他的父亲Sir John Lennard, II, Sheriff of Kent II29岁，母亲Lady Katherine Elizabeth Weston27岁。他于1526年与Elizabeth Harmon在英国肯特設維寧聖 波托爾福也斯 邱奇结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和7个女儿的父母。他于1570年住在英国英格兰肯特。他在英国英格兰倫敦被列为lawyer, barrister at the treasury’s office,  lincoln’s inn - exclusive society of lawyers。他去世于1590年3月12日在英国肯特設維寧享年82岁，埋葬在英国肯特設維寧聖 波托爾福也斯 邱奇。

nameMeanings.name: Leonard,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English; French (Léonard); Walloon (mainly Léonard): from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements leo ‘lion’ (a late addition to the vocabulary of ancient Germanic name elements, taken from Latin) + hard ‘hardy, brave, strong’, which was taken to England by the Normans. A Christian saint of this name, who is supposed to have lived in the 6th century, but about whom nothing is known except for a largely fictional life dating from half a millennium later, was popular throughout Europe in the early Middle Ages and was regarded as the patron of peasants and horses. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, e.g. Italian Leonardo , Polish, Slovenian, etc. Lenart or Lenard , and probably also their derivatives. Compare Larned , Learned , and Yenor .
Irish (Fermanagh): adopted as an English equivalent of Gaelic Mac Giolla Fhionáin or of Langan .
German: variant of   Leonhard  , cognate with 1 above.

variants.name: Leonardo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Manuel, Jose, Fernando, Carlos, Ernesto, Felipe, Guillermo, Julio, Luz, Mario, Alberto, Altagracia.  Italian Antonio, Angelo, Carmelo, Dario, Ercole, Filomena, Giuseppe, Marco, Mauro, Oreste, Pasco, Quintino.  Portuguese Joao, Paulo.
Italian, Spanish, and Portuguese: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements leo ‘lion’ + hard ‘hardy, brave, strong’ (see Leonard ).

variants.name: Langan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Declan.

Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Longáin ‘descendant of Longán’, a personal name probably derived from long ‘tall’, or perhaps from a homonymous long ‘ship’ (and so originally a byname for a seafarer). Sometimes Leonard is used as an equivalent.

variants.name: Learnard
meanings: Altered form of English   Leonard  . Compare   Learned  .

variants.name: Lenhard
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Otto, Hermann, Kurt, Urs.
Dutch and North German: from the personal name Leonhard (see Leonard ).

variants.name: Lenhart
meanings: North German, and Czech: from the personal name Leonhard (see Leonard , Lenart ).

variants.name: Lence
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Joaquin, Caridad, Carmella, Delfina, Domingo, Fidelia, Marta, Roberto, Sergio.
Galician: habitational name from any of the four places called Lence.
Slovenian (Lenče): from a pet form of the personal names Lenart (see Leonard ) and Magdalena (see Maudlin ).

variants.name: Lehn
meanings: German:
from a short form of the personal name Leonhard, see Leonard .
habitational name from any of various places of that name.

variants.name: Learned
meanings: Altered form of English   Leonard  . An intermediate stage in this change is   Learnard  . Compare   Larned  .

History: William Learned was admitted to the First Church in Charlestown, MA, in 1632, and in 1640 was one of the first settlers of Woburn, MA. One of his better known descendants, Ebenezer, a revolutionary war officer, was born at Oxford, MA, in 1728.

variants.name: Lenard
meanings: English and Irish; French (Lénard): variant of Leonard , French Léonard.

Polish, German, Croatian, and Slovenian; Hungarian (Lénárd); Slovak (also Lenárd and Lénárd): from local vernacular forms of the personal name Leonard (German Leonhard ). In North America, this surname may also be a shortened form of patronymics (and other derivatives) from this name, such as Croatian Lenardić and Slovenian Lenardič.

variants.name: Lenart
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Agnieszka, Bronislawa, Henryk, Henryka, Jacek, Janina, Leslaw, Stanislaw, Tadeusz, Witold, Zofia.

Polish, Czech, Slovenian, Croatian, and German; Slovak (Lenárt); Hungarian (Lénárt): from the personal name Lenart, Slovak Lenárt, Hungarian Lénárt, equivalents of Leonard (German Leonhard ). In North America, this surname may also be a shortened form of patronymics (and other derivatives) from this name, such as Polish Lenartowicz , Slovenian Lenarčič and Lenartič, Croatian Lenartić.

nameMeanings.name: Sir
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Old Norse, originally a byname meaning ‘bride, wife’; in mythology the name of Thor's golden-haired wife.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament: ‘God is gracious’. See John .

nameMeanings.name: Page
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Transferred use of the surname, a less common variant of Page, originally an occupational name given to someone who served as a page to a great lord. It was taken up as a girl's name in the 20th century in the United States and is now popular throughout the English-speaking world.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

en.lifeSummary: When Sir John Page Leonard, III, High Barrister at Lincoln Inn, Sheriff of Kent was born in 1508, in Chevening, Kent, England, United Kingdom, his father, Sir John Lennard, II, Sheriff of Kent II, was 29 and his mother, Lady Katherine Elizabeth Weston, was 27. He married Elizabeth Harmon in 1526, in St Botolph's Church, Chevening, Kent, England. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 7 daughters. He lived in Kent, England, United Kingdom in 1570. His occupation is listed as lawyer, barrister at the treasury’s office,  lincoln’s inn - exclusive society of lawyers in London, England, United Kingdom. He died on 12 March 1590, in Chevening, Kent, England, at the age of 82, and was buried in St Botolph's Church, Chevening, Kent, England.

fr.St Botolph's Church, Chevening, Kent, En: St Botolph's Church, Chevening, Kent, Angleterre
fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir John Page Leonard, III, High Barrister at Lincoln Inn, Sheriff of Kent est né en 1508, à Chevening, Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir John Lennard, II, Sheriff of Kent II, avait 29 ans et sa mère, Lady Katherine Elizabeth Weston, avait 27 ans. Il a épousé Elizabeth Harmon en 1526, à St Botolph's Church, Chevening, Kent, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 7 filles. Il a vécu à Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni en 1570. Sa profession de son indiquée est lawyer, barrister at the treasury’s office,  lincoln’s inn - exclusive society of lawyers à Londres, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Il est décédé le 12 mars 1590, à Chevening, Kent, Angleterre, à l’âge de 82 ans, et a été enterré à St Botolph's Church, Chevening, Kent, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir John Page Leonard, III, High Barrister at Lincoln Inn, Sheriff of Kent 1508 in Chevening, Kent, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir John Lennard, II, Sheriff of Kent II 29 und seine Mutter Lady Katherine Elizabeth Weston 27. Er hat 1526 in St Botolph's Church, Chevening, Kent, England Elizabeth Harmon geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 7 Töchter. 1570 hat er in Kent, England, United Kingdom gelebt. sein Beruf ist als lawyer, barrister at the treasury’s office,  lincoln’s inn - exclusive society of lawyers in London, England, United Kingdom aufgeführt. Er starb am 12. März 1590 im Alter von 82 Jahren in Chevening, Kent, England und wurde in St Botolph's Church, Chevening, Kent, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John Page Leonard, III, High Barrister at Lincoln Inn, Sheriff of Kent è nato il nel 1508 nel Chevening, Kent, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Sir John Lennard, II, Sheriff of Kent II, aveva 29 anni e il suo madre, Lady Katherine Elizabeth Weston, aveva 27 anni. Lui sposato Elizabeth Harmon nel 1526, nel St Botolph's Church, Chevening, Kent, England. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 7 figlie. Vissuto a Kent, Inghilterra, Regno Unito nel 1570. La sua occupazione è indicata come lawyer, barrister at the treasury’s office,  lincoln’s inn - exclusive society of lawyers nel London, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Morì il 12 marzo 1590, nel Chevening, Kent, England, all'età di 82 anni, e fu sepolto a St Botolph's Church, Chevening, Kent, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir John Page Leonard, III, High Barrister at Lincoln Inn, Sheriff of Kentが1508年にイギリスイギリスケントチェベニングで生まれた時，父Sir John Lennard, II, Sheriff of Kent IIは29歳，母Lady Katherine Elizabeth Westonは27歳であった。1526年にイギリスケントチェベニングスト ボトルフ'ス チャーチでElizabeth Harmonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と7人の娘の親であった。彼は1570年にはイギリスイギリスケントに住んでいた。イギリスイギリスロンドンでの職業はlawyer, barrister at the treasury’s office,  lincoln’s inn - exclusive society of lawyersと記録されている。彼は1590年3月12日にイギリスケントチェベニングで82歳で死去し，イギリスケントチェベニングスト ボトルフ'ス チャーチにて埋葬された。

ko.St Botolph's Church, Chevening, Kent, En: 영국 켄트 처베닝 스트 벗프'스 c후르치
ko.lifeSummary: Sir John Page Leonard, III, High Barrister at Lincoln Inn, Sheriff of Kent(이)가 1508년 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 처베닝에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir John Lennard, II, Sheriff of Kent II(은)는 29세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Katherine Elizabeth Weston(은)는 27세였다. 그는 1526년 영국 켄트 처베닝 스트 벗프'스 c후르치에서 Elizabeth Harmon(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 일곱 명의 딸을 두었다. 1570년도에 영국 잉글랜드 켄트에서 살았다. 그의 직업은 영국 잉글랜드 런던에서 lawyer, barrister at the treasury’s office,  lincoln’s inn - exclusive society of lawyers이었다. 그는 1590년 3월 12일 영국 켄트 처베닝에서 82세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 켄트 처베닝 스트 벗프'스 c후르치에 묻혔다.

pt.St Botolph's Church, Chevening, Kent, En: St Botolph's Church, Chevening, Kent, Inglaterra
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John Page Leonard, III, High Barrister at Lincoln Inn, Sheriff of Kent nasceu em 1508, em Chevening, Kent, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Sir John Lennard, II, Sheriff of Kent II, tinha 29 anos e sua mãe, Lady Katherine Elizabeth Weston, tinha 27 anos. Ele casou-se com Elizabeth Harmon em 1526, em St Botolph's Church, Chevening, Kent, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 7 filhas. Ele viveu em Kent, Inglaterra, United Kingdom em 1570. Sua profissão aparece como lawyer, barrister at the treasury’s office,  lincoln’s inn - exclusive society of lawyers em London, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Ele faleceu em 12 de março de 1590, em Chevening, Kent, Inglaterra, com 82 anos, e foi sepultado em St Botolph's Church, Chevening, Kent, Inglaterra.

ru.St Botolph's Church, Chevening, Kent, En: ст ботолф'с чурч, чевенинг, кэнт, Англия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir John Page Leonard, III, High Barrister at Lincoln Inn, Sheriff of Kent родился в 1508 г. в местности чевенинг, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Sir John Lennard, II, Sheriff of Kent II было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Katherine Elizabeth Weston, 27. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth Harmon в 1526 г. в местности ст ботолф'с чурч, чевенинг, кэнт, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 7 дочерей. Он жил в местности кэнт, Англия, Великобритания в 1570 году.  Он работал по профессии lawyer, barrister at the treasury’s office,  lincoln’s inn - exclusive society of lawyers в местности Лондон, Англия, Великобритания. Он умер 12 марта 1590 г. в местности чевенинг, кэнт, Англия в возрасте 82 лет и был похоронен в местности ст ботолф'с чурч, чевенинг, кэнт, Англия.

es.St Botolph's Church, Chevening, Kent, En: St Botolph's Church, Chevening, Kent, Inglaterra
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir John Page Leonard, III, High Barrister at Lincoln Inn, Sheriff of Kent nació en 1508, en Chevening, Kent, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Sir John Lennard, II, Sheriff of Kent II, tenía 29 años y su mamá, Lady Katherine Elizabeth Weston, tenía 27 años. Se casó con Elizabeth Harmon en 1526, en St Botolph's Church, Chevening, Kent, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 7 hijas. Vivió en Kent, Inglaterra, Reino Unido en 1570. Su ocupación registrada era lawyer, barrister at the treasury’s office,  lincoln’s inn - exclusive society of lawyers en London, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Murió el 12 de marzo de 1590, en Chevening, Kent, Inglaterra, a la edad de 82 años, y fue sepultado en St Botolph's Church, Chevening, Kent, Inglaterra.
